executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an extraordinary ransomware surge with 104 organizations publicly listed as victims in the past 48 hours, representing a coordinated mass attack across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board, and Peterborough Public Health. The 'Qilin' ransomware group alone claims 20 Canadian victims, while 'Clop' appears to be conducting a mass exploitation campaign with 13 victims. This represents a national-scale cyber emergency requiring immediate defensive action across public and private sectors.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 20 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, Traffic Tech, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Eastern Townships School Board, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), BioPharma Services, and multiple others across technology, healthcare, education, manufacturing, and professional services sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - double extortion ransomware operations with data leak site

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including strategic objectives consultancy, Ideal Welders, CMH Helicopters, law firm SMT Lawyers, Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, Whiskijack Resorts, and multiple technology and manufacturing firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) - known for mass exploitation of zero-day vulnerabilities in file transfer and enterprise software

items.name: Play
items.targets: 9 Canadian organizations including Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX Nutrition, Security ONE Alarm Systems, and accounting firm Pewarchuk CPA
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information) - sophisticated ransomware operation targeting manufacturing, technology, and professional services

items.name: Safepay
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Briway Carriers, educational institution Teccart, construction firms (Groupe Piche, Kenalex, Silverline Group), and manufacturing companies across Quebec and other provinces
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1571 (Non-Standard Port) - emerging ransomware group with focus on Canadian transportation and construction sectors

items.name: The Gentlemen
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including Groupe Caddac, ControlGMC (manufacturing), Labtician Ophthalmics (healthcare), and Real Tech
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol) - newer ransomware operation targeting Canadian manufacturing and healthcare

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including Phoenix Labs (technology), Esposito Bros. Construction, McKee-Pownall Equine Services (veterinary healthcare), Emond Publishing, CCR Solutions, and Soteck
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery) - aggressive ransomware group targeting construction and technology sectors

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including Active Green + Ross (automotive services), Morison Insurance Brokers (financial services), Kids & Company (childcare services), and The Sundher Group
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1087 (Account Discovery) - targeting consumer services and financial sectors

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health, KTL Group (technology), Laurysen Kitchens (manufacturing), Group Echo (business services), and Trucash
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing) - targeting public health and critical infrastructure

items.name: Incransom
items.targets: 7 Canadian organizations including Tract Consulting, construction firm Excavation Tourigny, Bison Family Medical (healthcare), Omega Tool Corp, and Steel Works Inc manufacturing facilities
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562 (Impair Defenses) - targeting healthcare and manufacturing with focus on smaller enterprises

items.name: AiLock
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations including Solutions Extreme Technology and Eeyou Communications Network (Indigenous telecommunications provider)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1074 (Data Staged) - targeting critical telecommunications infrastructure

items.name: Coinbase Cartel (and ShinyHunters)
items.targets: Canada Goose (Consumer Services) - targeted by both Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters groups simultaneously
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage) - double attack on major Canadian retail brand suggesting coordinated campaign or data sharing

items.name: Everest
items.targets: National Money Mart Company - database leaked (Financial Services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage) - confirmed data exfiltration with database leak affecting Canadian financial services

items.name: Anubis
items.targets: Markham Stouffville Hospital (Healthcare)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System) - critical healthcare infrastructure attack threatening patient care and privacy

items.name: Rhysida
items.targets: Collège Supérieur De Montréal (Education)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories) - targeting Quebec educational institution with potential student and staff data exposure

items.name: Worldleaks
items.targets: Municipality of North Perth (Public Sector)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System) - attack on municipal government infrastructure affecting public services

items.name: Akira
items.targets: Ecofit (industry unspecified)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021 (Remote Services) - established ransomware group expanding Canadian operations

items.name: Lapsus$
items.targets: Salesfloor (Technology)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - known for social engineering and credential theft targeting technology companies

items.name: Nightspire
items.targets: Regal Building Materials Ltd (Construction) and unidentified law firm
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) - targeting construction supply chain

items.name: Kairos
items.targets: Robbins Parking Service Ltd (Transportation/Logistics)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery) - targeting transportation infrastructure

items.name: Bravox
items.targets: John O's Foods (Agriculture and Food Production)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - targeting food supply chain

items.name: Sarcoma
items.targets: Propane Levac Inc. (Energy)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - targeting energy sector critical infrastructure

items.name: Nitrogen
items.targets: Whitfield Welding Inc (Manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - targeting specialized manufacturing

items.name: Securotrop
items.targets: Living Realty (Business Services), Cadman Power Equipment (Manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - emerging group targeting Canadian real estate and manufacturing

items.name: Medusa
items.targets: Thunder Bay Counselling (Public Sector)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1074 (Data Staged) - targeting mental health and social services with sensitive patient data

items.name: Devman
items.targets: Beauséjour Co-op (Agriculture), Axion50plus, and Ravand (Technology)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - targeting agricultural cooperatives and technology firms

items.name: Tridentlocker
items.targets: GuestTek (Technology - hospitality IT services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - targeting hospitality technology provider affecting multiple hotel chains

items.name: Payload
items.targets: Q2 Artificial Lift Services (Manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - targeting oil and gas equipment manufacturing

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 104 organizations publicly compromised in 48 hours across all provinces and territories, affecting every critical sector. Notable victims include major consumer brand Canada Goose (double-attacked), healthcare facilities Markham Stouffville Hospital and Thunder Bay Counselling, financial institution National Money Mart with confirmed database leak, public health agency Peterborough Public Health, municipal government North Perth, and educational institutions Eastern Townships School Board and Collège Supérieur De Montréal. No CCCS advisories were identified in this feed, suggesting potential intelligence gap during this mass-casualty cyber event.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose attacked simultaneously by Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters ransomware groups; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) compromised by Sinobi group
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak via Everest ransomware group; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi group; multiple accounting and financial services firms affected including Pewarchuk CPA and MG Chartered Professional Accountant

report_2w.executive_summary: Google has patched a Chrome zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-5281) that is actively being exploited in the wild—update immediately. North Korea's Lazarus Group compromised the widely-used Axios npm package in a supply chain attack that could affect thousands of downstream applications. The Canadian government issued an alert for a critical Citrix NetScaler vulnerability (CVE-2026-3055) requiring immediate patching. ShinyHunters claims to have breached Cisco Systems, allegedly leaking source code and Salesforce data. Additionally, Iran-linked Handala hackers targeted high-profile U.S. officials and Iranian dissidents through sophisticated phishing campaigns.

items.description: Critical vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway
items.software_affected: Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway

items.description: Chrome zero-day vulnerability with active in-the-wild exploitation
items.software_affected: Google Chrome (all versions prior to latest stable update)

items.name: Lazarus Group (UNC1069, North Korea APT)
items.targets: Axios npm package in supply chain attack affecting downstream JavaScript/Node.js applications globally
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software Supply Chain), T1071.001 (Web Protocols for C2), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Cisco Systems (alleged breach including source code and Salesforce data)
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: TeamPCP (North Korea-linked)
items.targets: AI startup Mercor, AWS environments via LiteLLM supply chain compromise
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software Supply Chain), T1078.004 (Cloud Accounts), T1580 (Cloud Infrastructure Discovery)

items.name: Handala (Iran-linked)
items.targets: Kash Patel (U.S. official), IranWire (Iranian dissident portal)
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Casbaneiro
items.targets: Latin America and Europe via phishing campaigns using dynamic PDF lures
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: FulcrumSec
items.targets: Unique Computing, ReFocus AI, Gennet AI (insurance sector breach exposing 23,000 policyholders, SSNs, driver licenses, ML models)
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1078.004 (Cloud Accounts), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage Object)

items.name: VEN0m Ransomware
items.targets: Organizations using BYOVD (Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver) technique with signed IObit driver to bypass Windows Defender
items.ttps: T1068 (Exploitation for Privilege Escalation), T1562.001 (Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-006 on March 30, 2026, warning of a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-3055) affecting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway products. This advisory requires immediate attention from Canadian organizations using these widely deployed enterprise networking solutions. No specific breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.
